# Clang Crash: Assertion Value > 0 failed in llvm::Align::Align due to negative vector_size attribute

**Author:** Theaamadu
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/165463
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** clang:frontend, crash-on-invalid, confirmed, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2025-11-03T18:27:13Z

## Body

## Compiler Explorer: https://godbolt.org/z/h18d7WfW9

- clang start crashing on 11.0.0
- generated by fuzzer

## Console
```console
clang++: /root/llvm-project/llvm/include/llvm/Support/Alignment.h:77: llvm::Align::Align(uint64_t): Assertion `Value > 0 && "Value must not be 0"' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace, preprocessed source, and associated run script.

## Code

typedef int vec32 __attribute__((vector_size(-8)));
vec32 foo(vec32 a, vec32 b) { return a - b; }
```
